report_name: 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers

threat_actors.name: Atomic Arch Actor
threat_actors.description: The unnamed threat actor group behind the 'Atomic Arch' supply chain attack, responsible for hijacking orphaned Arch User Repository (AUR) packages to distribute infostealers and rootkits to developers.
threat_actors.goals: Harvest developer credentials, tokens, and SSH keys for further compromise of corporate networks and other supply chain attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Attackers compromised the software supply chain by hijacking trusted but orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) and modifying their build scripts to install malware.

attack_patterns.name: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: The compromised `PKGBUILD` file, a shell script, was used to execute malicious commands, specifically `npm install`, to download the next stage payload.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The malicious npm package `atomic-lockfile` was transferred from the public npm registry to the victim's machine using the `npm install` command executed by the compromised build script.

attack_patterns.name: Private Keys
attack_patterns.description: The Rust-based infostealer payload was designed to specifically search for and exfiltrate SSH private keys from developer machines.

attack_patterns.name: Rootkit
attack_patterns.description: The final payload included an eBPF rootkit to hide the malware's processes and network connections, providing stealth and persistence.

malware.name: atomic-lockfile
malware.description: A malicious npm package used as a dropper in the Atomic Arch campaign. It is installed via a compromised PKGBUILD file and deploys a Rust-based infostealer and an eBPF rootkit.

malware.name: js-digest
malware.description: A malicious npm package used as a dropper in the Atomic Arch campaign, similar to atomic-lockfile.

malware.name: lockfile-js
malware.description: A malicious npm package used as a dropper in the Atomic Arch campaign, similar to atomic-lockfile.

malware.name: Atomic Arch Infostealer
malware.description: A Rust-based infostealer deployed by the Atomic Arch campaign, designed to search for and exfiltrate sensitive developer-related information like SSH keys, AWS credentials, and kubeconfigs.

malware.name: Atomic Arch eBPF Rootkit
malware.description: An eBPF rootkit deployed by the Atomic Arch campaign to provide stealth and persistence by hiding its processes and network connections.

tools.name: npm
tools.description: The Node Package Manager (npm) was abused by the attackers to host malicious packages and used as the installation mechanism on the victim's machine via compromised build scripts.

tools.name: Bun
tools.description: The Bun runtime was used as an alternative to npm for installing malicious packages in a second wave of the Atomic Arch campaign.

campaigns.name: Atomic Arch
campaigns.description: A supply chain attack campaign targeting Arch Linux developers by hijacking orphaned Arch User Repository (AUR) packages to distribute malware via malicious npm packages.

identities.name: Arch Linux Developers
identities.description: Developers using the Arch Linux operating system and its Arch User Repository (AUR) were the primary target of the Atomic Arch supply chain attack.

infrastructure.name: Malicious npm Registry Packages
infrastructure.description: The public npm registry was used to host malicious packages like 'atomic-lockfile', which served as the delivery vehicle for the final infostealer and rootkit payloads.

indicators.name: Atomic Arch Malicious npm Packages
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Atomic Arch Malicious npm Package File
id: 7c5d1e8a-9f0b-4c6d-8a3e-1b9c0f8d7e6a
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of malicious npm package files associated with the Atomic Arch campaign.
references:
  - https://www.sonatype.com/blog/atomic-arch-npm-campaign-adds-malicious-dependency
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '/atomic-lockfile/'
      - '/js-digest/'
      - '/lockfile-js/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of malicious npm packages associated with the Atomic Arch campaign.

indicators.name: Suspicious npm or Bun Install from Build Script
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious npm or Bun Install from makepkg
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4
status: experimental
description: Detects 'makepkg' or a shell process spawning 'npm' or 'bun' to install packages, a TTP used in the Atomic Arch campaign to hijack AUR packages.
references:
  - https://www.sonatype.com/blog/atomic-arch-npm-campaign-adds-malicious-dependency
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
  - attack.t1059.004
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '/makepkg'
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
    Image|endswith:
      - '/npm'
      - '/bun'
    CommandLine|contains: ' install '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a build script process (like makepkg) spawning a package manager (like npm or bun) to install packages, which is anomalous and indicative of the Atomic Arch TTP.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: Suspicious npm or Bun Install from Build Script
